title: ExpressVPN vs NordVPN in 2026: Which One Should You Choose?
description: ExpressVPN vs NordVPN tested head-to-head in 2026. Speed, price, privacy, and streaming compared so you can pick the right one for how you actually use a VPN.
content: ## ExpressVPN vs NordVPN 2026: The Full Breakdown These two VPNs show up at the top of almost every recommendation list. ExpressVPN has been around since 2009 and built its reputation on speed and reliability. NordVPN grew fast, added features aggressively, and now holds the largest server network of any commercial VPN. The question is not which one is good. Both are good. The question is which one fits how you actually use a VPN. ## Speed: ExpressVPN Wins on Long-Distance Routes ExpressVPN uses its own Lightway protocol, built on wolfSSL. On long-distance connections, US to Europe or Europe to Asia-Pacific, ExpressVPN consistently delivers faster speeds and lower latency than NordVPN in independent testing. NordVPN uses NordLynx, which is built on WireGuard. On nearby servers, same-country or neighboring country connections, NordLynx is fast and often slightly ahead of Lightway. The gap closes on short hops. Where the difference matters most: if you regularly connect to servers far from your physical location, ExpressVPN holds speed better. If you mostly connect to servers in your own region, the two are close enough that speed is not the deciding factor. ## Server Count: NordVPN Has the Larger Network NordVPN runs over 6,400 servers across 111 countries. ExpressVPN operates around 3,000 servers in 105 countries. More servers mean more options when a specific IP gets blocked by a streaming platform or when a particular location is congested. NordVPN's larger pool also means more redundancy. If one server is slow, you have more alternatives. ExpressVPN's smaller network is still substantial. For most use cases, you will not notice the difference. But for power users who frequently switch between specific countries or need reliable access in restrictive regions, NordVPN's breadth is an advantage. ## Price: NordVPN Is Cheaper NordVPN's long-term plan runs around $3 to $4 per month. ExpressVPN runs $6 to $8 per month for a comparable commitment. | | NordVPN | ExpressVPN | |---|---|---| | Monthly (pay monthly) | ~$12.99 | ~$12.95 | | Annual plan | ~$4.99/mo | ~$8.32/mo | | 2-year plan | ~$3.39/mo | ~$6.67/mo | | Simultaneous devices | 10 | 8 | | Money-back guarantee | 30 days | 30 days | At the 2-year price point, NordVPN is roughly half the cost of ExpressVPN. That gap adds up to $60 to $80 per year for essentially the same core functionality. ## Privacy: Both Pass the Audit Test Both VPNs operate under audited no-logs policies. Third-party auditors have reviewed both providers and confirmed that neither stores user activity, connection timestamps, or IP address logs. NordVPN is based in Panama, which sits outside Five Eyes, Nine Eyes, and Fourteen Eyes intelligence-sharing alliances. ExpressVPN is registered in the British Virgin Islands, also outside these alliances. NordVPN introduced RAM-only servers in 2019. Data on these servers is wiped on every reboot, making warrant-based data extraction technically infeasible. ExpressVPN has not moved its full network to RAM-only infrastructure. For most users, the privacy difference is negligible. Both have demonstrated in practice that they do not retain logs. For users with higher threat models, NordVPN's RAM-only infrastructure and Panama jurisdiction are slight technical advantages. ## Firestick and Netflix Compatibility Both VPNs unblock Netflix across multiple libraries. Both work on Amazon Firestick and Fire TV. Both have dedicated apps for Android TV and support sideloading where needed. For Firestick specifically, the installation process is identical: search for the app in the Amazon App Store, install it, log in. Both apps are well-designed and update regularly to stay ahead of streaming platform detection. For a full breakdown of which VPNs work best for streaming, see our guide on the best VPNs for Netflix and Firestick in 2026. ## Router Support: ExpressVPN Is Better Here ExpressVPN has the best router support of any VPN. It offers custom firmware for several popular router models including Asus, Netgear Nighthawk, and Linksys. Installing ExpressVPN at the router level protects every device on your network without installing the app on each one individually. NordVPN supports router use but does not offer custom firmware for consumer routers. Configuration requires manual setup using OpenVPN or NordLynx, which is not difficult but is less polished. If you want to run your VPN at the router level to cover smart TVs, gaming consoles, and other devices that cannot run VPN apps natively, ExpressVPN is the cleaner option. ## Who Should Choose Which **Choose NordVPN if:** - Price is a factor and you want strong performance for less - You connect to many different server locations and want maximum choice - You want RAM-only infrastructure as part of your privacy setup - You are a power user who uses features like Double VPN or Onion over VPN **Choose ExpressVPN if:** - You regularly connect to distant servers and speed is your priority - You want to install the VPN on your router with minimal technical setup - You value a polished app experience across all devices - Budget is not the primary concern If you are still deciding whether you need a VPN at all, the what is a VPN guide covers the basics and explains the practical scenarios where a VPN makes a real difference. ## The Verdict For most users, NordVPN is the better choice in 2026. The price difference over two years is significant, the server network is larger, and the performance on nearby servers is comparable to ExpressVPN. The gap only matters if you consistently connect to servers far from your physical location. ExpressVPN earns its price for router users and for anyone who regularly needs the fastest possible connection over long distances. The Lightway protocol is genuinely fast, the app quality is high, and the router firmware support is unmatched. Both VPNs offer a 30-day money-back guarantee. If you are unsure, try the one that better matches your primary use case and switch if it does not deliver.
